PRE CONDITIONS: Admin user logged into GCX
Steps:
1. Login as Admin 
 2. Navigate to Auto Streaming Settings page
Expected Result: GCX displays all Auto Streaming settings exactly as defined in Genesis

PRE CONDITIONS: Admin logged in, Auto Streaming page open
Steps:
1. Change a setting (e.g., enable/disable auto streaming)
2. Save changes
Expected Result: Changes are immediately reflected in Genesis; no GCX storage used

PRE CONDITIONS: Operator without admin permission logged in
Steps: 1. Navigate to Auto Streaming Settings page
Expected Result: Page or settings not accessible; proper error message displayed

PRE CONDITIONS: Admin logged in
Steps: 1. Open Auto Streaming Alarm configuration
Expected Result: GCX shows all Genesis-defined alarm types

PRE CONDITIONS: Admin logged in, Alarm config page open
Steps: 1. Select alarm(s) to trigger auto streaming                                2. Save
Expected Result: Only selected alarms trigger streaming; Genesis alarm definitions unchanged

PRE CONDITIONS: Admin attempts to create or edit alarm type
Steps:
1. Try to add or edit alarm type 
 2. Save
Expected Result: GCX does not allow creation/modification; error message shown

PRE CONDITIONS: Operator logged in, camera-sensor mapping exists
Steps: 1. Trigger qualifying alarm
Expected Result: Live stream starts automatically for relevant cameras

PRE CONDITIONS: Same as above
Steps: 1. Trigger alarm mapped to multiple cameras
Expected Result: All mapped cameras stream simultaneously

PRE CONDITIONS: Alarm is active, stream is live
Steps: 1. Close or acknowledge alarm
Expected Result: All streams stop according to Genesis rules

PRE CONDITIONS: Operator logged in
Steps: 1. Trigger alarm mapped to no cameras
Expected Result: No streams start; system stable

PRE CONDITIONS:
operator logged in 
- Zones defined in Genesis
 - Cameras mapped to zones
Steps: 1. Trigger an alarm mapped to a specific zone                        2.Observe which cameras start streaming
Expected Result: Only cameras in the alarm’s zone stream automatically Cameras outside the

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Default all cameras stream if no zones
PRE CONDITIONS: - Operator logged in - No zones defined in Genesis - Cameras exist
Steps:
1. Trigger an alarm    
 2. Observe cameras
Expected Result: All cameras stream automatically when no zones exist

PRE CONDITIONS: - Admin logged in - Site has zones defined in Genesis
Steps:
1. Open GCX 
2. Navigate to Zones page
Expected Result: Admin can view all site zones exactly as defined in Genesis

PRE CONDITIONS: - Operator logged in - No edit permissions - Site has zones
Steps: 1. Navigate to Zones page
Expected Result:
Zones displayed as read-only 
 Operator cannot modify

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Zone changes affect future alarms only
PRE CONDITIONS: Admin logged in - Site has zones - Some alarms active
Steps: 1. Change zone assignments in GCX                                           2.Trigger a new alarm
Expected Result: Changes affect new alarms only Existing alarms follow old zone config

PRE CONDITIONS:
- Admin logged in 
 - GCX Auto Streaming page open
Steps:
1. Open permissions settings 
2 . Modify roles allowed to stream 
3. Save
Expected Result: permissions are saved and applied Operators and Admins respect new settings

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Operator without permission cannot see streams
PRE CONDITIONS: operator logged in - Operator has no streaming permission
Steps:
1. Trigger alarm 
2. Check streaming page
Expected Result: Operator cannot see or access streams

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Streaming UI hidden for unauthorized roles
PRE CONDITIONS: - Operator without permission - GCX logged in
Steps:
1.Open GCX 
2. Navigate to streaming page
Expected Result: Streaming UI elements do not appear

PRE CONDITIONS:
- Operator without permission 
 - Attempt direct URL to stream
Steps: 1. Enter deep link URL
Expected Result:
Access denied 
 Operator cannot bypass UI restrictions

PRE CONDITIONS:
- Operator without permission 
 - Attempt direct or UI access
Steps:
1. Try to access streaming
 2. Check logs
Expected Result: Denied access recorded in audit logs

PRE CONDITIONS:
- Admin logged in
 - Alarm triggered
 - Cameras streaming
Steps:
1. Trigger alarm 
2. Observe streaming activity
 3. Check logs
Expected Result: Logs contain alarm ID, site, cameras streamed, user, timestamps

PRE CONDITIONS:
- Operator without streaming permission
 - Attempt to access streaming
Steps:
1. Try to open streaming UI or deep link
 2. Check logs
Expected Result: logs show denied access attempt with user, timestamp, and site

PRE CONDITIONS:
- Admin/Operator logged in
 - Trigger alarm
 - Force failure (simulate streaming fail)
Steps:
1. Trigger alarm 
2. Cause streaming failure
 3. Check logs
Expected Result: Logs include failure details: user, cameras, alarm ID, timestamp
